objective: Test whether a causal competing-risk decomposition of exact stop-loss, take-profit, and timeout paths improves after-cost expected-payoff ranking and fixed-policy economics over a matched direct conditional-mean control.

claims.reason: The 2025-H1 development interval has been consumed by prior rounds. Results are research evidence only, even if point estimates are positive.

round_49_diagnosis.finding: Profit probability retained moderate discrimination, while separately forecast gain and loss severities were highly collinear and their subtraction produced near-zero expected-net rank correlation in both calibration and evaluation.

prior_h60_derivative_feature_result.decision: Retain the 71 price/flow predictors for this isolated mechanism test; exact funding remains in every target and replay cash flow.

research_basis.finding_used: Competing outcomes should be modeled with calibrated event probabilities under proper scoring rules.
research_basis.limitation: Round 50 has no censoring because timeout is an observed terminal class, so it uses ordinary joint multinomial log loss rather than importing the paper's censoring adjustment.

research_basis.finding_used: Time-to-event forecasts require scoring rules that target the predictive distribution rather than an arbitrary concordance surrogate.
research_basis.limitation: Medical survival results are not financial-edge evidence.

research_basis.finding_used: Log likelihood is a proper distributional objective, while common ranking-style survival metrics need not be proper.
research_basis.limitation: Round 50 measures rank only as an out-of-sample diagnostic and does not train on a concordance objective.

research_basis.finding_used: Distributional target representations can stabilize noisy regression, motivating a future categorical payoff ablation if the simpler event decomposition is insufficient.
research_basis.limitation: Round 50 does not claim that classification universally dominates regression.

research_basis.finding_used: Categorical objectives can be more stable than scalar regression in noisy, non-stationary value estimation.
research_basis.limitation: The study is reinforcement learning; Round 50 is supervised and keeps direct mean regression as the matched control.

research_basis.finding_used: Forecast accuracy alone need not produce actionable trading performance, requiring a separate executable replay with costs.
research_basis.limitation: The paper's limit-order-book setting and reported values are not imported.

research_basis.finding_used: Walk-forward crypto evidence reinforces cost-aware abstention and evaluation after realistic friction.
research_basis.limitation: It is a recent preprint and cannot establish Round 50 profitability.

research_basis.finding_used: Independent deep seeds provide a practical disagreement signal for selective abstention.
research_basis.limitation: Disagreement is not a calibrated loss bound.

data_contract.sequence_gap_rule: No training or inference window may cross a missing five-minute timestamp or symbol boundary.
data_contract.second_level_limit: The local multi-year corpus is verified at one-minute resolution. The verified one-second aggregate-trade corpus spans only 2024-06-01 through 2024-06-07 and is reserved for later execution sensitivity; no multi-year second-level claim is permitted.

chronological_roles.role_boundary_rule: Entry, every path bar, exit, and every funding cash flow must remain inside one role.
chronological_roles.evaluation_use: One consumed-development mechanism screen only; no tuning after inspection.

target_contract.entry: The next verified minute open after each five-minute decision.
target_contract.stop_width: Trailing causal 60-minute one-minute-return volatility in basis points, multiplied by sqrt(60), then clipped to [24, 80] bps.
target_contract.funding: Exact settled funding events after entry and through exit, paid by longs and received by shorts for positive rates.
target_contract.intraminute_ambiguity: When both barriers lie inside one minute bar, fill the stop first.
target_contract.gap_through_stop: Fill at the minute open when it is worse than the stop; never assume a stop-price fill through a gap.
target_contract.timeout: Exit at the open exactly 60 minutes after entry when neither barrier was reached.
target_contract.risk_unit_target: Exact net payoff in bps divided by the causal stop width in bps.
target_contract.structured_residuals: For stop and take-profit separately, subtract the causal barrier payoff from exact risk-unit payoff and fit one training-only arithmetic-mean residual per symbol and side. These constants account for average funding and gap-through effects without a dynamic severity head.

model_contract.family: Per-symbol causal large-kernel temporal convolution with side-specific path-event and payoff heads.

candidates.action_value: Two direct conditional means of exact net payoff in risk units, trained by unweighted MSE.
candidates.shared_auxiliaries: Exact event-time multinomial log loss plus conditional timeout-profit BCE and timeout-payoff MSE.

candidates.action_value: Sum calibrated stop/take event probabilities times the causal barrier payoffs plus their training-only residual baselines, then add timeout probability times the conditional timeout-payoff mean.
candidates.single_primary_change_from_control: Remove direct mean regression and derive the mean from mutually exclusive path-event probabilities and the timeout conditional mean.

heads.event_time: Two independent 121-class logits, one for each side.
heads.timeout_profit: Two conditional logits scored only on timeout rows.
heads.timeout_mean: Two conditional risk-unit means scored only on timeout rows.
heads.direct_mean_control: Two risk-unit means present only in the direct candidate.

model_contract.candidate_selection: None. Both candidates complete all three seeds and the same evaluation.
model_contract.policy_uncertainty: Every seed must predict positive net value for eligibility; disagreement can only cause abstention.

training_contract.feature_scaling: Training-only per-feature median and scaled IQR, scale floor 1e-6, normalized feature clipping to [-12, 12].
training_contract.target_scaling: Divide exact net payoff by the decision-time stop width; no fitted target transformation and no target clipping.
training_contract.optimizer: Explicit DirectML-compatible AdamW, learning rate 0.0008, weight decay 0.0001, gradient norm clip 1.0.
training_contract.compute: DirectML required for the live host run; CPU is a portability mode only.
training_contract.short_screen: Numerical preflight and one-seed training/early-stop diagnostics may run before the full experiment; calibration and evaluation economics cannot be inspected or used to alter the frozen design.

calibration_contract.event_method: One positive temperature per seed and candidate, shared across symbols, sides, and event classes, fitted on calibration multinomial log loss.
calibration_contract.timeout_profit_method: One bounded positive-slope Platt transform per seed and candidate, shared across symbols and sides.

numerical: All losses, outputs, gradients, probabilities, and parameters are finite.
The DirectML preflight records zero CPU-fallback warnings.
All six checkpoints and prediction artifacts reload and hash-verify exactly.
Every event distribution sums to one within 1e-5.
event: Positive evaluation multinomial-log-loss skill versus training event-time prevalence for each side.
Positive evaluation event Brier skill versus training event prevalence for stop, take-profit, and timeout for each side.
At least four of six evaluation months have positive event log-loss skill for each side.
Minimum pairwise seed expected-payoff Spearman correlation of 0.50.
action_value: Positive expected-payoff MSE skill versus the training conditional mean for each side.
Minimum expected-payoff Spearman correlation of 0.03 for each side.
Positive expected-payoff Spearman correlation for all three symbols and at least four evaluation months for each side.
The competing-risk candidate improves average side Spearman over the direct control by at least 0.005 without degrading relative MSE by more than 1%.

fixed_policy_contract.entry_rule: For a flat symbol, a side is eligible only when all three seeds predict strictly positive exact after-cost expected payoff. Choose the side with the greater worst-seed payoff; exact ties abstain.
fixed_policy_contract.exit_rule: Use the exact target's stop, take-profit, or timeout exit. Do not overlap positions in the same symbol.
fixed_policy_contract.base_sleeve: Fixed one-third unlevered notional sleeve per symbol.
fixed_policy_contract.stress_rule: Reprice the identical base trade ledger at 16 bps without reselecting timestamps or sides.
fixed_policy_contract.leverage_sensitivity: Only after an unlevered candidate passes every quality and economic gate, replay the identical ledger at 5x, 10x, and 15x with margin, liquidation-distance, portfolio drawdown, and loss-budget gates. Leverage cannot rescue a failed unlevered candidate.

rejected_models.fino1:8b: Failed the liquidation case because leverage was omitted from the rationale.
rejected_models.fin-r1:8b: Understated post-reconnect uncertainty and failed the required risk-score range.

ai_contract.permitted_role: Asynchronous schema-constrained veto after deterministic model, data, ownership, execution, and risk gates pass.
ai_contract.uplift_test: Run only if a deterministic candidate passes. Compare paired chronological baseline and AI-veto daily net returns under identical costs; require positive exact sign-test and moving-block-bootstrap lower bounds, no worse drawdown or profit factor, and complete artifact hashes.

required_graphs: dated daily equity versus cash baseline
monthly net return and trade count
publication_contract.graph_source_rule: Every graph is generated from a tracked machine-readable table; hand-edited or synthetic financial graphics are forbidden.
publication_contract.negative_result_rule: Zero trades, negative returns, failed gates, concentration, and uncertainty must be reported prominently and cannot be relabeled as success.
